learning_outcomes.description: Identify and interpret relevant building standards and codes.
Apply basic structural principles to a given design project.
Select and detail appropriate building products and construction systems.
Independently prepare a set of technical drawings.
Collaboratively prepare a partial construction model from a set of technical drawings.

assessments.assessment_title: QUIZ: Construction techniques, materials and codes
assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale:

The quiz will test knowledge of construction techniques, principles of building structures and construction codes based on lectures from week 1 to week 7 and selected texts.

In-person assessment not permitting use of AI or MT: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: Exams submitted after the end of the submission time will incur a late penalty.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.assessment_title: A01A: Construction Drawing Set (GA only, work In progress)
assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale:

A01A: Construction Drawing Set (GA only, work in progress) requires students to prepare a basic "general arrangement" set of drawings to AS1100.301, based on an existing architectural design. As a minimum, A01 submissions must include a 1:200 site plan, 1:100 floor and reflected ceiling plans for each level, 1:100 roof plan, 2 x 1:100 sections and 2 x 1:100 elevations. All drawings are to include model information generated by 3D elements supported by annotation in the form of 2D elements (lines, fills, text etc) that indicate compliance with construction codes and standards, specifies structural and construction materials.

A01A: Construction Drawing Set is to be submitted to Blackboard as a single PDF file.

Complex / authentic assessment using AI and/or MT to support learning: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The School of Architecture, Design and Planning uses Blackboard and Turnitin for assessment submission. Turnitin is accessed through the course Blackboard site. Turnitin also checks for plagiarism or instances where the original work of others is not appropriately acknowledged. Uploaded files must contain readable text and not be rasterised. Students are advised to commence assignment uploads with sufficient amount of time (consider possible technical problems with computers, internet speed, etc). After successfully submitting an assignment through Blackboard or Turnitin, a ‘Submission Complete!’ screen will be displayed. It is the student’s responsibility to check assignment preview and confirm successful submission. If the ‘Submission Complete!’ screen isn’t displayed, the student should regard the submission as unsuccessful. Students should download a copy of the digital receipt as proof they have submitted the assignment. Students who are experiencing upload issues must advise the Course Coordinator immediately by email and should include screenshots and a copy of the assessment for submission. To meet professional accreditation, public engagement and quality assurance obligations, digital copies of all course assessment items must be submitted in addition to any hard copy submission requirements specified in individual Course / Studio Outlines.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: The late penalty for this assessment item will be calculated as follows:
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: A01B: Review and assessment of A01A submissions
assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale:

A01A: Construction Drawing Set (GA only, work in progress) submissions by all students will be pinned up for review, giving all students a chance to see the range of work produced to this point. Students are to complete an assessment of their own work and the work of another student under simulated exam conditions as a part of the identity verification process for the course.

Assessment tasks prohibiting use of AI or MT: Complex / authentic assessment using AI and/or MT to support learning: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: A4 paper copies of the assignment rubric and assignment feedback sheets will be provided to students. Students are to complete an assessment of their own work and that of another student by hand, and to submit their assessments to the course coordinator before leaving the pin up and review venue.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale:

A02: Construction Drawing Set (Final) builds on the previous drawing assignment. Students have the opportunity to refine and improve the general arrangement drawings submitted for A01A: Construction Drawing Set (GA only, work in progress) and are required to develop key construction detail drawings based on their A01 submission.

In addition to the site plan, 1:100 floor and reflected ceiling plans for each level, 1:100 roof plan, 2 x 1:100 sections and 2 x 1:100 elevations required for A01, A02 submissions must also include:

  • a 1:20 wall section running from footings up to roof of the design,
  • diagrams of the construction sequence based on the 1:20 wall section, and
  • 2 x 1:5 construction details drawn from the 1:20 wall section which specify the materials involved and explain how the building's structure, cladding and lining are connected to form the building envelope.

Complex / authentic assessment using AI and/or MT to support learning: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The School of Architecture, Design and Planning uses Blackboard and Turnitin for assessment submission. Turnitin is accessed through the course Blackboard site. Turnitin also checks for plagiarism or instances where the original work of others is not appropriately acknowledged. Uploaded files must contain readable text and not be rasterised. Students are advised to commence assignment uploads with sufficient amount of time (consider possible technical problems with computers, internet speed, etc). After successfully submitting an assignment through Blackboard or Turnitin, a ‘Submission Complete!’ screen will be displayed. It is the student’s responsibility to check assignment preview and confirm successful submission. If the ‘Submission Complete!’ screen isn’t displayed, the student should regard the submission as unsuccessful. Students should download a copy of the digital receipt as proof they have submitted the assignment. Students who are experiencing upload issues must advise the Course Coordinator immediately by email and should include screenshots and a copy of the assessment for submission. To meet professional accreditation, public engagement and quality assurance obligations, digital copies of all course assessment items must be submitted in addition to any hard copy submission requirements specified in individual Course / Studio Outlines. Any physical models should be photographed and a minimum of two photographs describing the complete and full model must be included in the digital submission.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 12(23 Feb - 24 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Lecture

Lectures will run weekly on Friday mornings from week 1 through to week 12, with the exception of Week 6 which is the Good Friday public holiday.

Lectures cover important course content around building materials, construction techniques and structural principles and will make most sense if you have completed the required readings before class.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Lecture Lectures will run weekly on Friday mornings from week 1 through to week 12, with the exception of Week 6 which is the Good Friday public holiday. Lectures cover important course content around building materials, construction techniques and structural principles and will make most sense if you have completed the required readings before class.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 12(23 Feb - 24 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Tutorial

Tutorials will run weekly on Friday mornings from week 1 through to week 12, with the exception of Week 6 which is the Good Friday public holiday.

Tutorials in weeks 1-5 and week 7 will be focused on learning the fundamentals of ArchiCAD or Revit (your choice) and will run for two hours.

Tutorials in weeks 8, 9 and 11 will be focused on finalising your drawing set and preparing details and you will be assigned to a specific one hour session

The tutorial in week 10 will be a pin up and review session where you can compare your work to the work of other students and gain insights into how to improve your drawings.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Tutorial Tutorials will run weekly on Friday mornings from week 1 through to week 12, with the exception of Week 6 which is the Good Friday public holiday. Tutorials in weeks 1-5 and week 7 will be focused on learning the fundamentals of ArchiCAD or Revit (your choice) and will run for two hours. Tutorials in weeks 8, 9 and 11 will be focused on finalising your drawing set and preparing details and you will be assigned to a specific one hour session The tutorial in week 10 will be a pin up and review session where you can compare your work to the work of other students and gain insights into how to improve your drawings.
